tree.setup: A tree-lined street with old buildings and laundry hanging from balconies. You're trying to find a park but your phone has no signal. An elderly man, Uncle Wu, is sitting on a folding stool outside a tea shop, watching the world go by. His cane leans against the wall. He's been sitting in this exact spot every afternoon for twenty years.
tree.setup_zh: 一条两边有树的老街，阳台上晾着衣服。你想找公园，但手机没信号。吴叔叔坐在茶馆门口的折叠凳上看风景。他的拐杖靠在墙上。他每天下午都坐在这个位置，已经二十年了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_en: Young person! What place are you looking for?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask for directions to the park.

options.text_en: Excuse me, where is the park?
options.feedback: Uncle Wu's face brightens. He loves giving directions -- it's the highlight of his afternoon.「往前走五分钟。」请问 (qǐngwèn) before a question is polite and opens doors.
Uncle Wu waves his hand dismissively.「不远不远！走路十分钟。」The A不A pattern (远不远) is a great way to ask yes-or-no questions.
Uncle Wu stands up with surprising energy. He's been waiting for someone to help all afternoon.「来，我告诉你。」迷路 (mí lù) means 'to get lost' -- useful when you need it, which will be often.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 往前走，到路口右拐，就看到了。
turns.text_pinyin: wǎng qián zǒu, dào lùkǒu yòu guǎi, jiù kàndào le.
turns.text_en: Walk forward, turn right at the intersection, and you'll see it.

options.feedback: Uncle Wu waves you off with a gentle smile. Using 您 (nín) instead of 你 shows respect for elders -- he noticed, and he appreciates it.
Uncle Wu nods and settles back into his stool. He'll be here tomorrow if you get lost again. 再见 is 'goodbye' -- literally 'see you again.'
Quick but missing the thank-you. Uncle Wu watches you go, mildly deflated. When someone gives you help, a 谢谢 makes it complete.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: màn zǒu a! gōngyuán hěn hǎokàn de!
turns.text_en: Take care! The park is beautiful!

tree.cultural_note: Elderly people sitting outside their neighborhood shops are often the best source of local directions. Using 您 (nín) instead of 你 (nǐ) when addressing older people is a simple way to show respect. 慢走 (màn zǒu, 'walk slowly') is a warm farewell.
